title: Look Down, O Lord, From Heaven Behold
music: from Erfurt Enchiridion, 1524 Karl August Haupt, 1869

lyrics.v1: Look down, O Lord, from heav'n behold, And let thy pity waken! How few the flock within thy fold, Neglected and forsaken! Almost thou'lt seek for faith in vain, And those who should thy truth maintain Thy Word from us have taken.
lyrics.v2: With frauds which they themselves invent Thy truth they have confounded; Their hearts are not with one consent On thy pure doctrine grounded; And, whilst they gleam with outward show, They lead thy people to and fro, In error's maze astounded.
lyrics.v3: God surely will uproot all those With vain deceits who store us, With haughty tongue who God oppose, And say, "Who'll stand before us? By right or might we will prevail; What we determine cannot fail, For who can lord it o'er us?"
lyrics.v4: For this, saith God, I will arise, These wolves my flock are rending; I've heard my people's bitter sighs To heav'n my throne ascending: Now will I up, and set at rest Each weary soul by fraud opprest, The poor with might defending.
lyrics.v5: The silver sev'n times tried is pure From all adultera- tion; So, through God's word, shall men endure Each trial and temptation: Its worth gleams brighter through the cross, And, puri- fied from human dross, It shines through every nation.
